violations.description: Test flow branches on runtime option counts, creating multi-path behavior that can hide deterministic failures.
violations.suggestion: Split each branch into explicit scenarios so each test has one deterministic path.

violations.description: Make-call flow branches on option availability instead of enforcing explicit fixture preconditions.
violations.suggestion: Use fixed fixture states or separate tests for "with options" and "without options" paths.

violations.description: Runtime timestamps are generated in signature tests; this is mostly safe but can reduce exact reproducibility in edge timing windows.
violations.suggestion: Prefer fixed timestamps for deterministic replay-oriented crypto/signature scenarios where feasible.

violations.description: Dynamic timestamp/number generation is used in integration tests, introducing slight nondeterministic variance.
violations.suggestion: Use deterministic seeded values in scenarios where uniqueness can be handled by fixture setup.

recommendations: Split conditional E2E branches into deterministic scenario-specific tests.
Use fixture-preconditioned data instead of branching on discovered UI option counts.
Prefer fixed timestamps for replay-sensitive signature validations where practical.
summary: Determinism is strong overall with 4 violations (2 MEDIUM, 2 LOW).
